meaning: adj.不舒服的,被强迫的,拘泥的
examples: Research has been constrained by a lack of funds.
She felt constrained from continuing by the threat of losing her job.
The evidence was so compelling that he felt constrained to accept it.
For the salary-cap-constrained Knicks, re-signing the 36-year-old Prigioni should become a bigger priority now.
Should an objecting pharmacist have their moral compass constrained because of where they chose to practice?
